## Publish citywide and community-level qualityof-life metrics on equity and sustainability by 2023

The City will work with community groups and frontline leaders to identify metrics that meaningfully reflect community needs, aspirations, and well-being. These metrics will be measured and publicly reported every year.

policy_statement: City of Chicago aims to reduce city GHG emissions by a minimum of 62% by 2040 (using 2017 inventory levels as baseline).
verbatim_text:
The 2022 CAP aims to chart an equitable path to reduce Chicago's GHG emissions by a minimum of 62% by 2040.

Guided by the strategies and actions of this plan, the City of Chicago aims to reduce the city's level of GHG emissions by 62% by 2040, based on 2017 GHG inventory levels.
section_header: GHG REDUCTION TARGETS / ACCOUNTABILITY AND IMPLEMENTATION
extraction_rationale: Citywide quantified emissions reduction target with deadline explicitly stated.

policy_statement: Chicago will not count offset credits toward achieving its 62% GHG reduction goal.
verbatim_text: Chicago will not count offset credits toward its 62% GHG reduction goal achievement. Furthermore, the City will reduce its own emissions before taking action beyond its borders.
section_header: The Role of Offsets
extraction_rationale: Explicit policy decision regarding accounting rules for the stated GHG target.

policy_statement: To kickstart implementation of the climate strategy, the City is investing $188 million in equity-focused interventions spanning building retrofits, expansion of the air quality monitoring network, and planting over 75,000 trees.
verbatim_text: To kickstart the implementation of my administration's climate strategy, we are investing $188 million in equity-focused interventions, spanning building retrofits, the expansion of the air quality monitoring network, to the planting of over 75,000 trees.
section_header: Letter from the MAYOR
extraction_rationale: Explicit funding commitment with a quantified amount and specified intervention areas.

policy_statement: As part of the 2022 budget, the City has committed significant funding to strengthen existing, introduce new, and anticipate future initiatives; results will inform future climate policies.
verbatim_text: As a part of the 2022 budget, the City has committed significant funding to strengthen existing, introduce new, and anticipate future initiatives. The Climate Financing and Delivery Capacity section provides more detail about the historic climate action investments the City has made. Results from these investments will inform future climate policies, and strong performance will build momentum for more funding.
section_header: Investing in Climate Equity
extraction_rationale: Budgetary resource allocation commitment (though not fully quantified here) tied to implementing initiatives and informing future policy.

policy_statement: City will establish community-led climate advisory boards and forums for regular community participation in implementing, reporting, and achieving the 2022 CAP actions.
verbatim_text: The City will also establish community-led climate advisory boards and forums for regular community participation in implementing, reporting, and achieving the actions related to the 2022 CAP.
section_header: Governance and Reporting
extraction_rationale: Clear governance/participation mechanism the City commits to establish.

policy_statement: City departments and sister agencies will explore, share, and publicly track incremental targets to keep pace with the 2022 CAP vision.
verbatim_text: These departments will explore, share, and publicly track incremental targets that will keep pace with the vision of the 2022 CAP.
section_header: Governance and Reporting
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to public tracking of incremental targets (accountability mechanism).

policy_statement: Support a 3-year review and revision cycle of the 2022 CAP targets.
verbatim_text:
Our strategy will include:

- Supporting a 3-year review and revision cycle of the 2022 CAP targets
section_header: ACCOUNTABILITY AND IMPLEMENTATION
extraction_rationale: Clear commitment to an ongoing review cycle with specified periodicity.

policy_statement: Formalize the 2022 CAP actions through legislation and policy co-designed with local stakeholders.
verbatim_text:
Our strategy will include:

- Formalizing the plan's actions through legislation and policy co-designed with local stakeholders
section_header: ACCOUNTABILITY AND IMPLEMENTATION
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to formalize actions via legislation and co-design process.

policy_statement: Leverage and/or optimize the City's racial equity impact assessment tools for equitable implementation of climate actions.
verbatim_text:

- Leveraging and/or optimizing the use of the City's racial equity impact assessment tools for equitable implementation of climate actions
section_header: ACCOUNTABILITY AND IMPLEMENTATION
extraction_rationale: Specific implementation commitment using an established tool for equity assessment.

policy_statement: Install new community compost collection hubs in neighborhoods underserved by organics collection services ($45 thousand).

policy_statement: Invest in municipal fleet electrification combined with EV charging, prioritizing fleet electrification in pollution-burdened neighborhoods ($8.3 million).
verbatim_text: City Fleet and Building Decarbonization | $8.3 million | Invest in municipal fleet electrification combinedwithelectric vehicle charging. This program will prioritize fleet electrification in pollution-burdened neighborhoods.

extraction_rationale: Funding allocation with specific intervention and equity prioritization.

policy_statement: Expand bike and micromobility infrastructure and walkability investments in priority communities, providing 5,000 bikes, helmets, and locks to underserved Chicagoans ($10 million).
verbatim_text: Low-Carbon Mobility Projects | $10 million | Expansion of bike and micromobility infrastructure and walkability investments in priority communities. This project will provide 5,000 bikes, helmets, and locks to underserved Chicagoans.
section_header: Chicago Recovery Plan funding overlap with Chicago's 2022 Climate Action Plan
extraction_rationale: Funded program with a quantified deliverable (5,000 items) and specified intervention type.

policy_statement: Invest in a citywide air monitoring network and partial remediation of the former Schroud Superfund site ($24 million).

section_header: Chicago Recovery Plan funding overlap with Chicago's 2022 Climate Action Plan
extraction_rationale: Funding allocation with explicit deliverables (monitoring network and remediation).

policy_statement: As part of the Chicago Recovery Plan, City Council approved $188 million for climate resiliency and mitigation (October 2021).
verbatim_text: In October 2021, Chicago's City Council approved $188 million for climate resiliency and mitigation, as part of Mayor Lori Lightfoot's $2.5 billion Chicago Recovery Plan.
section_header: CLIMATE FINANCING AND DELIVERY CAPACITY
extraction_rationale: Formal approval of a quantified funding amount for climate purposes.

policy_statement: Tree equity goal: plant 75,000 trees by 2026.
verbatim_text: Together with city departments and community partners, Chicago has the goal of planting 75,000 trees by 2026.
section_header: TREE EQUITY
extraction_rationale: Quantified tree-planting goal with deadline stated explicitly.
